description:
GOAL: Either resurrect src/aot.rs (currently #![cfg(any())]-gated) or
build a new AOT path on top of ir_codegen. AOT is the third execution
path per the three-path parity requirement; today it's dark.

Discovered 2026-05-11 during the .19 concurrency arc that AOT support
had been deferred since fz-ul4.11.9 ("legacy direct-style codegen
retired; preserved verbatim for intent verification once ir_codegen
reaches feature parity in .11.10-.11.14"). The .11 arc reached feature
parity; AOT resurrection was implied but never explicitly scheduled.

## Two paths to evaluate

A. Resurrect src/aot.rs. It was a .12-era direct-style codegen with no
   GC, no closures, no heap types. To fit current fz it would need to
   be rewritten on top of ir_codegen — at which point "resurrect" is a
   misnomer; this is mostly building a new AOT.

B. Build a new AOT driver on top of ir_codegen. Same Cranelift code as
   the JIT, but emit a relocatable object instead of in-memory JIT.
   Link with fz_runtime + libc + a startup constructor that initializes
   the runtime (atom table, eventual Process/Runtime startup).

## Architectural questions

1. Does AOT support concurrency (spawn/send/receive)? If yes, the
   compiled binary needs a Runtime baked in or linked in. The
   fz_runtime staticlib is the natural home for this.

2. What's main()? For a single-task program, AOT can run
   `CompiledModule::run(main_entry)` equivalent at startup. For
   multi-task (spawn), it needs a Runtime::new(...).spawn(main).
   run_until_idle() entry path.

3. Heap allocation: the JIT path's HEAP is per-Process via
   CURRENT_PROCESS TLS. AOT inherits this directly if we link the same
   runtime fns.

## Out of scope for this ticket

The full AOT build pipeline (object emission, linking with cc, ELF/
Mach-O artifacts) is substantial. This ticket scopes the DECISION and
the minimum-viable path; implementation lands in sub-tickets.

## Deliverables

- Design doc: decide between (A) and (B). Likely (B).
- Either: prototype a "hello world" AOT binary that runs `fn main(),
  do: 42` and exits with code 42 (no concurrency yet); OR detailed
  design notes for the AOT pipeline.
- A test that the same fixture passes via the AOT path.

## Dependencies

- Blocks: fz-ul4.19.8 (three-path parity test runner needs all three
  paths).

## Acceptance

- Either: a working AOT path that compiles + runs at least one
  fixture; OR a design ticket detailing the AOT pipeline and sub-
  tickets to build it.
- One commit (or a tree of sub-commits if AOT is split into pieces).
title: AOT path resurrection (foundational for three-path parity)
